title: Refactor SRP-003 (SRP): The class combines input stream management from `DataInput`, JSON token trave...

detected_issue.description: The class combines input stream management from `DataInput`, JSON token traversal, various JSON value parsing (strings, numbers, booleans, null), JSON name parsing and canonicalization, Base64 decoding, character encoding/decoding, whitespace/comment skipping, and error reporting.
detected_issue.reasoning: This class exhibits the same fundamental SRP violations as `UTF8StreamJsonParser`. It integrates low-level `DataInput` reading, the core JSON parsing state machine, specific logic for parsing different JSON value types, complex UTF-8 character decoding and escape sequence handling, Base64 decoding, and error reporting. For example, `_parseUnsignedNumber()` (number parsing), `_parseName()` (name parsing), `_decodeBase64()` (Base64 decoding), and `_decodeUtf8_2()` (UTF-8 decoding) are all tightly coupled within this single class. This makes it highly susceptible to changes from multiple, unrelated domains.

fix_logic.summary: Extracts Base64 decoding logic from UTF8DataInputJsonParser into a dedicated helper class.
fix_logic.rationale: The `UTF8DataInputJsonParser` class was responsible for both JSON parsing and Base64 decoding from a `DataInput` source, violating the Single Responsibility Principle. This refactoring extracts all Base64 decoding methods (`_readBinary`, `_decodeBase64Escape`, `_handleBase64MissingPadding`, `_reportInvalidBase64Char`) into a new helper class, `UTF8DataInputBase64Decoder`. This new class encapsulates the specific logic for reading and decoding Base64 from a `DataInput`, improving modularity and reducing the `UTF8DataInputJsonParser`'s reasons to change. The parser now delegates Base64 operations to this new component, maintaining existing behavior and public API.
